title: The '<UNK>' Token Explosion
concept: If your vocabulary threshold is too high, too many words turn into '<UNK>', stripping the text of all specific meaning.

exercises.prompt: In natural language processing, what does the '<UNK>' token stand for?
exercises.explain: The '<UNK>' token stands for 'Unknown'. It is used to represent words that are not in the AI's official vocabulary list.

cards.title: Mystery Recipe
cards.body: Imagine reading a recipe that says: "Mix the <UNK> with the <UNK> and bake it in the <UNK>." Sounds impossible to cook, right? This is what happens to an AI when we throw away too many words!

cards.title: What is the '<UNK>' Explosion?
cards.body: When building a text AI, we choose a cutoff (threshold) for how popular a word must be to keep it. If we set this threshold too high, only a tiny handful of words are kept. Everything else is labeled as '<UNK>' (Unknown), causing a massive loss of information.

cards.title: The Tiny Dictionary
cards.body: Imagine translating a beautiful storybook using a tiny dictionary that only contains 50 common words. Every time you see a word like 'dragon', 'wizard', or 'castle', you have to write 'THINGS'. Soon, the epic tale reads like: 'The THINGS went to the THINGS to fight the THINGS.' The magic is completely lost!

cards.title: A Dangerous Over-Simplification
cards.body: If your threshold requires a word to appear 1,000 times, rare but critical words like 'allergic', 'penicillin', or 'danger' are wiped out. A medical warning like 'Warning: Patient is allergic to penicillin' becomes 'Warning: Patient is <UNK> to <UNK>'. This is a dangerous loss of detail!

cards.title: Is Cleaning Always Better?
cards.body: Some believe that replacing rare words with '<UNK>' is always good because it cleans up the data. While it does keep the vocabulary size small and tidy, doing it too aggressively destroys the core meaning of your text, rendering the AI useless.

cards.title: Detail vs. Blank Space
cards.body: See how raising the threshold too high washes away the unique identity of your sentences, making different thoughts look identical to the AI.

right.text:
Everything becomes unknown:
"I saw a <UNK> and a <UNK> at the <UNK>."

left.text:
Keep specific words:
"I saw a T-Rex and a Triceratops at the museum."

exercises.prompt: What happens if we set our vocabulary threshold too high?

options.text: The AI learns new words much faster.
Too many words are turned into '<UNK>' tokens, destroying the meaning.
The dictionary size grows too large to fit in memory.
All sentences are automatically translated to English.

exercises.explain: A high threshold means a word must appear very frequently to be kept. This filters out too many words, replacing them all with '<UNK>'.

exercises.prompt: Setting a very high vocabulary threshold is a great way to improve an AI's understanding of rare topics.
exercises.explain: While a small vocabulary saves computer memory, setting the threshold too high removes the specific words needed for the AI to understand the text.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing token name that replaces rare words when the threshold is too high.
exercises.sentence: If a word is too rare for our threshold, the system replaces it with the <UNK> token.
exercises.explain: When a word is not common enough to pass our vocabulary threshold, the AI replaces it with '<UNK>' (Unknown), hiding its true meaning.

exercises.prompt: Which of these messages has suffered from an '<UNK>' token explosion?

options.text: "Your order of pizza is on the way."

exercises.explain: The message containing '<UNK>' tokens has lost all the specific words ('pizza', 'pepperoni') that tell us what is actually being delivered.

exercises.prompt: You are building a chat assistant, but you set the vocabulary threshold so high that 90% of the words are replaced with '<UNK>'. Why is this a major disaster for training your model?

options.text: It makes the AI process sentences much faster without losing any details.
It trains the AI to guess the missing words perfectly.
It replaces almost all unique details with '<UNK>', leaving no useful context for the AI to learn.
It automatically translates foreign words into your native language.

exercises.explain: Setting a vocabulary threshold too high acts like a giant eraser, replacing all the unique and meaningful details of your dataset with generic '<UNK>' tokens. This leaves the model with no real context to learn from.
